critic.verdict: Technically synchronized but highly repetitive, relying on a lazy cyclic lane pattern and awkward rhythmic gaps.

issues.problem: Skipping the strong third beat of the measure (e.g., at 7.47s, 10.19s, and 12.92s) while keeping beats 1, 2, and 4 feels empty and rhythmically awkward for a synthwave track.
The lane placement follows a rigid, repeating 'BAR -> GAP -> NOTE' (Center -> Left -> Right) cycle throughout the entire chart, ignoring melodic shape and drum positioning.
There are zero sustain holds mapped despite the track's prominent, long synth pads, making the gameplay feel too one-dimensional.

design_notes: S0 (Intro): Simple 2-beat intervals introduce players to the three lanes sequentially starting with a center kick. S1 (Drop): Density increases with a flowing alternating lane pattern using 1-beat and 2-beat intervals to match the rising energy. S2 (Outro): Rhythms settle back down to steady 2-beat intervals, providing a breathing, cyclic outro that feels smooth and satisfying to finish on.

design_sections.name: S0
design_sections.intent: Introduce basic lane positions at a very relaxed pace.

design_sections.name: S1
design_sections.intent: Increase density with rhythmic flow and clean, alternating movements.

design_sections.name: S2
design_sections.intent: Cool down with steady, spaced-out taps to let the chart breathe.
